title: R5-series qualifying residential site - alternative maximum base height and building height (section 23-424)
description: DRAFT extraction (needs_review) of the ALTERNATIVE (taller) height envelope available to all four R5 variants (R5, R5A, R5B, R5D) on a QUALIFYING RESIDENTIAL SITE per NYC Zoning Resolution section 23-424 (City of Yes, Last Amended 2024-12-05): maximum base height 45 ft, maximum building height 55 ft, with a setback per section 23-423. FAIL-CLOSED INPUT: applicability is conditional on the section 12-10 qualifying-residential-site test (>= 5,000 sf lot within the Greater Transit Zone with wide-street/short-block frontage and no excluding district) - geography the platform CANNOT evaluate from the canonical property_profile. qualifying_residential_site is therefore a REQUIRED input with no canonical source; when missing/unavailable the evaluation returns professional_review_required with NO value. This alternative COMPETES with the base-district height rules (r5-height / r5b-height / r5d-height, and the r5a pitched envelope) for the same max_base_height / max_building_height decision; when both are simultaneously applicable the same-family conflict detector surfaces a rule_conflict and NO value is selected. NOT a Verified determination.

citations.quote: R5 R5A R5B R5D | Maximum Base Height 45 | Maximum Height of Buildings or other Structures 55

A setback shall be provided in accordance with this Section. The depth of the setback shall be at least 10 feet from a street wall fronting on a wide street, and at least 15 feet from a street wall fronting on a narrow street.

inputs.name: zoning_district
inputs.description: Residence district governing the lot. Applicability is in_set R5/R5A/R5B/R5D; a non-R5 district is a visible not_applicable.

inputs.name: qualifying_residential_site
inputs.description: True iff the lot meets the section 12-10 'qualifying residential site' test. REQUIRED and FAIL-CLOSED: the test turns on lot area + Greater-Transit-Zone geography + frontage/short-block conditions + district exclusions that the canonical property_profile CANNOT establish, so in practice this is unavailable and the evaluation returns professional_review_required with no value. The alternative envelope is never assumed to apply.

inputs.name: overlay_present
inputs.description: True iff zoning.commercial_overlays[] is non-empty; when true -> professional_review_required (section 23-44).

inputs.name: special_district_present
inputs.description: True iff zoning.special_districts[] is non-empty; when true -> professional_review_required (section 23-44).

outputs.name: max_base_height
outputs.description: Alternative maximum base height (ft) on a qualifying residential site. Separate typed constraint; not a buildable-envelope result. Competes with the base-district max_base_height.

outputs.name: max_building_height
outputs.description: Alternative maximum building height (ft) on a qualifying residential site. Separate typed constraint; not a buildable-envelope result. Competes with the base-district max_building_height.

parameters.name: qrs_base_height_ft
parameters.note: Section 23-424 qualifying-residential-site maximum base height 45 ft (R5/R5A/R5B/R5D). Candidate value pending raw-HTML verification and G6 approval.

parameters.name: qrs_building_height_ft
parameters.note: Section 23-424 qualifying-residential-site maximum building height 55 ft (R5/R5A/R5B/R5D). Candidate value pending raw-HTML verification and G6 approval.

steps.note: Alternative maximum base height on a qualifying residential site (section 23-424).
Alternative maximum building height on a qualifying residential site (section 23-424).

exceptions.description: The section 23-424 alternative envelope requires a setback per section 23-423, whose depth is street-width conditional (10 wide / 15 narrow, section 12-10). That depth is a separate street-width-gated constraint (rule r5-setback) and is not restated here.
A commercial overlay may modify residence-district height (section 23-44); the value is not emitted as final. Professional review required.
A mapped special district may modify height (section 23-44); the value is not emitted as final. Professional review required.

uncertainty_policy.note: An uncertain, split, sliver, or invalid M2-T013 geometric district assignment is never collapsed into a single definitive district: coverage downgrades to professional_review_required (or data_conflict).

limitations: Draft extraction pending raw-HTML source verification and G6 qualified-human legal approval; not a Verified determination.

Whether a lot is a 'qualifying residential site' (section 12-10) is a separate legal determination the platform cannot make from the canonical profile; without qualifying_residential_site the rule fails closed to professional_review_required.

This alternative competes with the base-district height rules for max_base_height / max_building_height; a same-family rule_conflict is surfaced rather than a silent precedence pick when both are applicable.
The section 23-423 setback for this envelope is a separate street-width-gated constraint (rule r5-setback).
